primary_diagnosis.name: Crohn's disease flare with enteropathic (peripheral) arthritis and dehydration

pathophysiology_summary: Loss of biologic cover (missed dose) precipitates a Crohn's flare with mucosal inflammation causing colicky pain and secretory diarrhoea, and an immune-mediated peripheral (type 1) enteropathic arthritis producing warm, fluctuant, asymmetric large-joint effusions that track gut activity. Ongoing fluid loss causes dehydration and tachycardia. The dual-system flare is the leading explanation, but warm effusions in an immunosuppressed patient make septic arthritis a genuine not-to-miss requiring aspiration, and the abdominal picture must be re-examined for obstruction/perforation. Telehealth cannot examine the abdomen or joints, aspirate a joint, take bloods, or give IV therapy — so the correct action is urgent in-person/ED assessment with rehydration and joint aspiration, not home systemic steroids.

differential.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
differential.evidence_basis: Simultaneous GI and large-joint flare after a missed biologic is the classic pattern.
Warm fluctuant effusions in an immunosuppressed patient must be aspirated to exclude infection.
Crohn's predisposes to strictures/obstruction; must screen for rigidity and vomiting.
Crystal arthritis is possible but the polyarticular large-joint pattern tied to IBD is atypical.

differential.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare
differential.evidence_basis: Soft abdomen with stool/flatus and no single septic-looking joint favour an enteropathic flare, pending aspiration.
Lower probability but not excludable without aspiration in an immunosuppressed patient.
Less likely with passage of stool/flatus, but re-examine for evolving obstruction.
Distribution and IBD linkage argue against gout; crystals on aspiration would settle it.

differential.diagnosis: Enteropathic arthritis secondary to Crohn's flare with dehydration
differential.evidence_basis: Working telehealth diagnosis requiring urgent in-person assessment: aspirate joints to exclude sepsis, rehydrate, take bloods, and restart/adjust biologic under specialist care.

red_flags.description: A single joint becoming fiery red and maximally painful, or high spiking fever/rigors (septic arthritis)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Septic arthritis in an immunosuppressed patient destroys joints and causes sepsis; needs urgent aspiration.

red_flags.description: Rigid board-like abdomen or faeculent vomiting (surgical abdomen/obstruction)
Progressive dehydration with tachycardia while isolated and unable to reach fluids
red_flags.clinical_significance: Volume depletion risks pre-renal AKI and collapse, worsened by isolation.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Hypovolaemic collapse and acute kidney injury.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Accepting the dual flare and failing to arrange joint aspiration to exclude septic arthritis in an immunosuppressed patient.
Prescribing systemic steroids over chat before sepsis/surgical abdomen are excluded, risking masked/worsened infection.
Missing tachycardia/dry mucosa and the inability to rehydrate at home while isolated.
Assuming a soft abdomen now excludes an evolving obstruction/perforation without arranging re-examination.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Clinically dehydrated with compensatory tachycardia; needs IV rehydration and bloods
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Supports urgent in-person/ED assessment rather than remote management.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Joint examination and aspiration/synovial fluid analysis
Bloods (FBC, CRP, U&E, cultures) and objective hydration/renal status
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Arrange urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment for joint aspiration, bloods, IV rehydration and specialist-guided steroid/biologic decisions; escalate to 000 for a rigid abdomen, faeculent vomiting, a single fiery joint, or collapse.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Home systemic steroids or reassurance could mask septic arthritis or a surgical abdomen and leave a dehydrated, isolated patient to deteriorate.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Severe IBD flares with systemic features require in-hospital assessment; steroid initiation should follow exclusion of infection and be specialist-guided.
An acutely swollen joint, especially in an immunosuppressed patient, requires urgent aspiration to exclude septic arthritis before immunosuppressive treatment.
